lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Blub! Blub...where is my Juliet? Have you seen her?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Looking for a blonde girl, goes by the name of Juliet..quite pretty...haven't seen her have you?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Juliet, Juliet, wherefore art thou Juliet? Have you seen my Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh woe is me that I cannot find my Juliet! You haven't seen Juliet have you?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Sadness surrounds me now that Juliet's father forbids us to meet. Have you seen my Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: What is to become of me and my darling Juliet, I cannot find her anywhere, have you seen her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have seen her actually!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At least, I think it was her... Blonde? A bit stressed?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Golly...yes, yes...you make her sound very interesting!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: And I'll bet she's a bit of a fox!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess she could be considered attractive...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: I'll bet she is! Wooooooooo!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Sorry, all that jubilation has made me forget what we were talking about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were asking me about Juliet? You seemed to know her?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yes, Juliet!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: The fox...could you tell her that she is the love of my long and that I life to be with her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Surely you mean that she is the love of your life and that you long to be with her?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yeah...what you said...tell her that, it sounds much better! Oh you're so good at this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, okay. I'll let her know.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh great! And tell her that I want to kiss her a give.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you want to give her a kiss!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh you're good...you are good!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: I see I've picked a true professional...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that I can try and find her for you!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ah yes....quite right. Hmmm, let me think now.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: She may still be locked away at her Father's house on the sest vide of Warrock.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, I remember how she loved it when I would sing up to her balcony! She would reward me with her own personal items...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, she just gave you her stuff?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well, not exactly give...more like 'throw with considerable force'...she's always a kidder that Juliet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else you can tell me about Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, there is so much to tell...she is my true love, we intend to spend together forever...I can tell you so much about her..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ermmm.....

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: So much can I tell you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes..

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: So much to tell...why, where do I start!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes..yes! Please go on...don't let me interrupt...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ermmm.....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't remember can you?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Not a thing sorry....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry Romeo, I've got better things to do right now. Maybe later?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, okay, well, I guess my Juliet and I can spend some time apart. And as the old saying goes, 'Absinthe makes the heart glow longer'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you mean that, 'Absence makes the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually forget it...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, I haven't seen her.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh...well, that's a shame...I was rather hoping you had.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why? Is she a fugitive? Does she owe you some money or something?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Hmmm, she might do? Perhaps she does? How do you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know? I was asking 'YOU' how 'YOU' know Juliet!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ahh, yes Juliet, she's my one true love. Well, one of my one true loves! If you see her, could you tell her that she is the love of my long and that I life to be with her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Surely you mean that 'she is the love of your life and that you long to be with her?'

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yeah...what you said...tell her that, it sounds much better! Oh you're so good at this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I can help find her for you? What does she look like?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh would you? That would be great! She has this sort of hair...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hair...check..

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: ...and she these...great lips...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lips...right.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh and she has these lovely shoulders as well..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shoulders...right, so she has hair, lips and shoulders...that should cut it down a bit.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yes, Juliet is very different...please tell her that she is the love of my long and that I life to be with her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Surely you mean that 'she is the love of your life and that you long to be with her?'

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yeah...what you said...tell her that, it sounds much better! Oh you're so good at this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, remember me?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Of course, yes....how are.. you....ermmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You haven't got a clue who I am do you?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Not a clue my friend, but you seem to have a friendly face...a little blood stained, and perhaps in need of a wash, but friendly none the less.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You asked me to look for Juliet for you!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ah yes, Juliet...my sweet darling...what news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing so far, but I need to ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What are you doing here? Snooping around...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to see Juliet on Romeo's behalf.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What...what...Romeo! Why that good for nothing swine...he's always trying to get the affections of my daughter..that soppy, half brained nincompoop won't ever have the heart of my daughter.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: She deserves someone of character, wit and repose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so wrong about Romeo?

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Wrong! What's wrong with him...have you actually talked to him? He's nothing but a dim witted upperclass twit, totally useless.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: If he threw a stone at the ground, he'd probably miss! He's totally invisible when it's raining because he's so wet!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: If you started with what's right with him, you'd have much less to consider!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I admit, he's probably not the sharpest knife in the cutlery draw...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Sharp? I've seen keener wit in root vegetables. Anyway, stop changing the subject. Get out of here and don't think you can sneak up those stairs to see Juliet, because I'll catch you and then you'll be for it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That seems a bit harsh....

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Harsh but fair I think you'll find...now get OUT!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've just come to have a chat with Juliet.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What on earth about? I hope you're not in cahoots with that good for nothing Romeo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err..no of course not....why would I be?

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: He's been trying to wooo my daughter for an age. Up until now she's had the good sense to just ignore him. I just don't know what's gotten into her recently so that she would give him the time of day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, love is mysterious! Perhaps one day someone may even learn to love you!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What! Someone may fall in love with me...what are you trying to insinuate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...Nothing....I guess I'd better be going now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...just looking around...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Just looking around! This is MY house! You might have at least 'ASKED' to view my considerably well appointed abode...but no, you've just burst in with all the elegance of a Troll at a tea party.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see that you're busy ranting so I'll just nip off and investigate a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Hi, I'm Phillipa, Juliet's cousin. I like to keep an eye on her, make sure that dashing young Romeo doesn't just steal her away under our plain old noses!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: He'd do it, you know - he's ever so dashing, and cavalier, in a wet blanket sort of way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Juliet, I come from Romeo. He begs me to tell you that he cares still.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh how my heart soars to hear this news! Please take this message to him with great haste.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I hope it's good news...he was quite upset when I left him.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: He's quite often upset...the poor sensitive soul. But I don't think he's going to take this news very well, however, all is not lost.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Everything is explained in the letter, would you be so kind and deliver it to him please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly, I'll do so straight away.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Many thanks! Oh, I'm so very grateful. You may be our only hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Juliet!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Hello there...have you delivered the message to Romeo yet? What news do you have from my loved one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I've not had chance to deliver it yet!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh, that's a shame. I've been waiting so patiently to hear some word from him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, that's the thing about messages...they're so easy to misplace...

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: How could you lose that message? It was incredibly important...and it took me an age to write! I used joined up writing and everything!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Please, take this new message to him, and please don't lose it.

sections.title: Talking to Phillipa after agreeing to deliver the message

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Oh, hello. Juliet has told me what you're doing for her and Romeo, and I have to say I'm very grateful to you. Juliet deserves a bit of happiness in her life.

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: And I'm sure Romeo is just the sort of jester to make her laugh out loud - hysterically you might say.

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: He always brings a tear to my eyes - tears of happiness at his foolish antics!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, thanks. I like to do my cupid bit.

sections.title: Talking to Draul Leptoc after agreeing to deliver the message

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What are you doing in my house? Up to no good I shouldn't wonder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just a small chore for Juliet, you do have a lovely daughter in her sir.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Oh...why, thank you...I've always tried to my best...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: ...Hang on! Enough of that smiley talk. I have a daughter and I know what she's like. Don't even think of carrying on anything behind my back, I have the eyes of a hawk, nothing gets past me!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Hey! What's that in your hands...looks like a message to me...with Juliet's barely legible scrawl on it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes, that's probably why I can't read it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I mean, that's right sir. I'm just popping to the shops to get some groceries for Juliet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, have to be off now...thanks...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Groceries!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Groceries!...at a time like this, does that girl know what she's putting me through!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Romeo...great news...I've been in touch with Juliet!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh great! That is great news! Well done...well done... what a total success!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and she gave me a message to give you...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ohhh great! A message....wow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: A message...oh, I can't wait to read what my dear Juliet has to say....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know...it's exciting isn't it...?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Yes...yes...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: You've lost the message haven't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, haven't got a clue where it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Romeo...great news...I've been in touch with Juliet! She's written a message for you...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, a message! A message! I've never had a message before...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: No, no, not one!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, well, except for the occasional court summons.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: But they're not really 'nice' messages. Not like this one! I'm sure that this message will be lovely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well are you going to open it or not?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yes, yes, of course! 'Dearest Romeo, I am very pleased that you sent [player name] to look for me and to tell me that you still hold affliction...', Affliction! She thinks I'm diseased?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Affection?'

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ahh yes...'still hold affection for me. I still feel great affection for you, but unfortunately my Father opposes our marriage.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear...that doesn't sound too good.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: What? '...great affection for you. Father opposes our..

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: '...marriage and will...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: ...will kill you if he sees you again!'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to be honest, it's not getting any better...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: 'Our only hope is that Father Lawrence, our long time confidant, can help us in some way.'

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well, that's it then...we haven't got a chance...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about Father Lawrence?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: ...our love is over...the great romance, the life of my love...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...or you could speak to Father Lawrence!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, my aching, breaking, heart...how useless the situation is now...we have no one to turn to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: FATHER LAWRENCE!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Father Lawrence?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh yes, Father Lawrence...he's our long time confidant, he might have a solution! Yes, yes, you have to go and talk to Lather Fawrence for us and ask him if he's got any suggestions for our predicament?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Father Lawrence?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Lather Fawrence! Oh he's...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: You know he's not my 'real' Father don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I suspected that he wasn't.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well anyway...he tells these song, loring bermons...and keeps these here Carrockian vitizens snoring in his church to the East North.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Not so good my friend...I miss Judi..., Junie..., Joopie...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Juliet! I miss Juliet, terribly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, so I see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Father Lawrence?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Lather Fawrence! Oh he's...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: You know he's not my 'real' Father don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I suspected that he wasn't.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well anyway...he tells these song, loring bermons...and keeps these here Carrockian vitizens snoring in his church to the East North.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard anything from Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Sadly not my friend! And what's worse, her Father has threatened to kill me if he sees me. I mean, that seems a bit harsh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I shouldn't worry too much...you can always run away if you see him...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: I just wish I could remember what he looks like! I live in fear of every man I see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Using a duplicate message on Romeo

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Why are you waving that around at me as if it's something important?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey again Romeo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Juliet, I have passed your message on to Romeo..he's scared half out of his wits at the news that your father wants to kill him.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Yes, unfortunately my father is quite the hunter, you may have seen some the animal head trophies on the wall. And it would be so awful to see Romeo's head up there with them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what you mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...his hair colour will clash terribly with the rest of the decoration.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: That's not what I was suggesting at all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, I know...I was just kidding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, don't worry because I'm on the case. I'm going to get some help from Father Lawrence.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh yes, I'm sure that Father Lawrence will come up with a solution. I hope you find him soon.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Do you live here? If so, how's about a couple of hundred gold towards the rent eh? Pay your share I say...you don't want to be like that freeloading Romeo!

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: ''...and let Saradomin light the way for you... '' Urgh!

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Can't you see that I'm in the middle of a Sermon?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Romeo sent me!

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: But I'm busy delivering a sermon to my congregation!

lines.speaker: Congregation
lines.text: Zzzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, well, it certainly seems like you have a captive audience!

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Ok, ok...what do you want so I can get rid of you and continue with my sermon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Romeo sent me. He says you may be able to help.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Ah Romeo, yes. A fine lad, but a little bit confused.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, very confused....Anyway, Romeo wishes to be married to Juliet! She must be rescued from her father's control!

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: I agree, and I think I have an idea! A potion to make her appear dead...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dead! Sounds a bit creepy to me...but please, continue.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: The potion will only make Juliet 'appear' dead...then she'll be taken to the crypt...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Crypt! Again...very creepy! You must have some strange hobbies.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Then Romeo can collect her from the crypt! Go to the Apothecary, tell him I sent you and that you'll need a 'Cadava' potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apart from the strong overtones of death, this is turning out to be a real love story.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Ah, have you found the Apothecary yet? Remember, Cadava potion, for Juliet.

sections.title: Talking to Romeo after Father Lawrence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Romeo!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ooh did you manage to survive one of Lather Fawrences sermons? I bet not, you were ages! I bet you snoozed on the welcome mat just as soon as you heard his voice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did not!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, come on, come on, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wants me to go to the Apothecary!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: The Apothecary?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh...is he the one who mixes up all them magical potion- ey things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I think so...but the word potion-ey doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well, you just used it...so I guess it does exist!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It doesn't matter...do you know where the Apothecary is?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Why should I tell you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because I'm doing you a favour!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Right, yes...of course! Well, I think the potion-ey place is Wouth Sest of here and near a sword shop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems very keen for you to marry Juliet.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Me too! I can't wait! Do you think it will be soon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, but I'll do what I can. It just seems a bit odd that Father Lawrence is so keen for you to marry?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: I can't imagine why he's so keen? Though, he was our messenger before you. Of course, he had a lot more hair back then...and I think we may have gone to school together...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, since you were kids?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Yeah! Thinking about it now, we called him 'Diddy Dorrence' back then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, maybe, he just wants a bit of peace? And when you two are married, he won't have to traipse around delivering your messages anymore?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Yes, the years haven't been good to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Juliet after Father Lawrence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Juliet, I've found Father Lawrence and he has a cunning plan. But for it to work, I need to seek the Apothecary!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh good! I knew Father Lawrence would come up with something. However, I don't know where the apothecary is...I hope you find him soon. My father's temper gets no better.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apothecary, Father Lawrence sent me. I need a Cadava potion to help Romeo and Juliet.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Cadava potion. It's pretty nasty. And hard to make.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Wing of rat, tail of frog. Ear of snake and horn of dog.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I have all that, but I need some Cadava berries.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: You will have to find them while I get the rest ready. Bring them here when you have them. But be careful. They are nasty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Conveniently, I have some here.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Phew! Here is what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do these berries look like?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: They're a nice bright pink colour, with green leaves, stalks are a pale brown colour...you can't miss them. They look pretty tasty, but I wouldn't advise eating them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get these berries?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I think I saw some recently in the local vicinity. Oh yes, that's right! There was a little bit of commotion when some kids visited the south eastern Varrock mining pit. The trip was disrupted because some kids

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: had found some cadavaberry bushes and they started picking them. They wrapped that trip up pretty quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are these berries dangerous?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Hmm, they're generally okay, but if handled improperly they can be a bit nasty. And I certainly wouldn't advise drinking the potion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Drinking the potion at the Apothecary's

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urk!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Blimey... you've been out for hours! Are you ok? People were tripping over you as they walked into the shop.

sections.title: Attempting to drink the potion elsewhere

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Keep searching for those Cadavaberries. They're needed for the potion.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Well done. You have the berries.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Phew! Here is what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the Cadava potion.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: You're welcome. I hope it helps that young couple find happiness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I gave Juliet the Cadava potion.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Good, good. I hope it helps that young couple find happiness.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Did you find the Apothecary?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I did. He's told me I must find some Cadava berries.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Well, good luck with that...they're quite tricky to find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any clues where I can start to look?

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: I heard some kids saying they saw some the other day. They were visiting the mining place to the south east of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that's as good a place to start looking as any.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Did you find the Apothecary?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the cadava potion.

lines.speaker: Father Lawrence
lines.text: Good! Good work! Ok, take it to Juliet, she's expecting you. I'll talk to Romeo and make sure he knows what to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Romeo!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh hello, have you seen Lather Fawrence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he's given me details of a potion which should help resolve this situation. The Apothecary is helping me prepare it.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ooooh, it all sounds horribly complicated?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: All I can say is I'll be glad when Juliet's finally in that crypt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spoken like a true lover!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ooohh, that's the potion is it? Rather you than me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not drinking it! It's for Juliet!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Well, I mean...it's probably delicious...and not dangerous at all. And you quite often see harmless drinks in a glowing sort of pink colour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I'm not convinced...maybe you ought to try some before I give it to Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Urgh! No way! Not in a million years!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok...but you know what to do when I've given this to Juliet?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, yeah...sure Lather Fawrence explained all that to me...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Hey , what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing much sir...I promise...I'm just an innocent friend of Juliet's...doing her a few favours...as a friend.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Well, just make sure there's no funny business going on that's all I can say. Do you know why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I can guess sir...

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: There's no need to guess! You can see it in my hawk like eyes, in my cat like ears and my dog like nose...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you saying that you look like an animal sir?

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: NO! Not look like an animal! I have the keen 'SENSES' of an animal and I don't miss a thing. Don't even think about trying anything!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Hey! What's that in your hands? Looks like some sort of potion to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...no! Nope! Not a potion! Some medicine...I have a terrible cough ...cough...cough....see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: >Except, I can't drink too much or else it makes me feel 'really' tired. One sip of this and the lights go out...I mean, I'm asleep in a minute.

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Sleep!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Sleep...! How can you even think of such a thing at a time like this? And I hope you have no intentions of sleeping in my house!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: You'll get a bill for rent if I catch you dozing off around here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Juliet!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Hi [player name], how close am I to being with my true love Romeo?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I still have to get a special potion for you.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh, I hope it isn't a love potion because you would be wasting your time. My love for Romeo grows stronger every minute...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That must be because you're not with him...

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh no! I long to be close to my true love Romeo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, ok then...I'll set about getting this potion as quickly as I can!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Fair luck to you, the end is close.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Juliet! I have an interesting proposition for you...suggested by Father Lawrence. It may be the only way you'll be able to escape from this house and be with Romeo.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Go on....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a Cadava potion here, suggested by Father Lawrence. If you drink it, it will make you appear dead!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Yes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And when you appear dead...your still and lifeless corpse will be removed to the crypt!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oooooh, a cold dark creepy crypt...

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: ...sounds just peachy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then...Romeo can steal into the crypt and rescue you just as you wake up!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: ...and this is the great idea for getting me out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be fair, I can't take all the credit...in fact...it was all Father Lawrence's suggestion...

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Ok...if this is the best we can do...hand over the potion!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Wonderful! I just hope Romeo can remember to get me from the crypt.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Please go to Romeo and make sure he understands. Although I love his gormless, lovelorn soppy ways, he can be a bit dense sometimes and I don't want to wake up in that crypt on my own.

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Oh, here's Phillipa, my cousin...she's in on the plot too!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: She's going to make it seem even more convincing!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Yes, I'm quite the actress! Good luck dear cousin!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Right...bottoms up!

lines.speaker: Juliet
lines.text: Urk!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Oh no...Juliet has...died!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You might be more believable if you're not smiling when you say it...

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Oh yeah...you might be right...ok, let's try again.

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Oh no...Juliet has...died?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps a bit louder, like you're upset...that your cousin has died!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Right...yes...Ok, ok, I think I'm getting my motivation now. Ok, let's try this again!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: OH NO...JULIET HAS...DIED?.... Oooooohhhhhh....(sob), (sob).Juliet...my poor dead cousin!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: What's all that screaming?

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: Oh no! My poor daughter...what has become of you?

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Poor Juliet...make preparations for her body to be placed in the Crypt...

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Oh, hello again! How was I - do you think I was convincing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yes, totally!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Ohhh good! I do hope Juliet will be pleased as well! That dashing young Romeo must be beside himself with excitement! Have you told him the good news yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but soon!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Good, good - you lucky thing, I can't wait to see the look on his face!

lines.speaker: Draul Leptoc
lines.text: My poor Juliet....she's dead...dead! I shouldn't have been so hard on her...she was my lovely daughter. Booo hoooo hooooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Romeo, it's all set. Juliet has drunk the potion and has been taken down into the Crypt...now you just need to pop along and collect her.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ah right, the potion! Great...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: What potion would that be then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Cadava potion...you know, the one which will make her appear dead! She's in the crypt, pop along and claim your true love.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: But I'm scared...will you come with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, ok...come on! I think I saw the entrance when I visited there last...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: This is pretty scary...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, be quiet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're here. Look, Juliet is over there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You go over to her...and I'll go and wait over here...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ohhh, ok then...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Hey...Juliet...

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Juliet....?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh dear...you seem to be dead.

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: Hi Romeo...I'm Phillipa!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Wow! You're a fox!

lines.speaker: Phillipa
lines.text: It's a shame about Juliet...but perhaps we can meet up later?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Who's Juliet?
